venue: Thurman Springs Park, 4485 Pineview Street, Powder Springs
blurb:
⭐ **Featured Event: Powder Springs Seafood Festival**
📅 Friday, May 15 | All day | Thurman Springs Park, Powder Springs
🎟️ Free admission | [Learn More](https://www.powderspringsga.gov/916/Powder-Springs-Seafood-Festival)

Seafood, live entertainment, artisan vendors, and family-friendly fun all in one spot, and it does not cost a thing to walk in. The Powder Springs Seafood Festival takes over Thurman Springs Park in downtown Powder Springs on Friday, with fresh seafood options and market vendors alongside the music. It is a quick shot west from East Cobb and a solid way to start the weekend on a Friday when you do not want to cook. Bring the family or make it a casual outing with friends.
scoring_notes: Seafood festivals have broad appeal for this income bracket. Free entry keeps it low-friction. Powder Springs is a short drive from East Cobb. The family-friendly format works for the established-family segment of the audience. Lower uniqueness score because food festivals are common, but the specific seafood theme and all-day format give it enough distinction to include. Best as a secondary pick or 'also happening this weekend' note rather than the featured event.

blurb:
⭐ **Featured Event: ImportExpo**
📅 Saturday, May 16 | 5:00–10:00 PM | Cobb Galleria Centre, Cumberland
🎟️ Free | [Learn More](https://www.importexpo.net)

If there is a car person in your house (and in East Cobb, there usually is), Saturday evening just got sorted. ImportExpo at the Cobb Galleria Centre brings out a diverse lineup of luxury cars and specialty vehicles, with vendors and live entertainment alongside the show floor. Free to get in, runs until 10 PM, and gives you something genuinely interesting to walk around and look at. Bring the teenager who just got their license, or the neighbor who spent all spring talking about their dream car. This one is easy.
scoring_notes: Car culture skews well toward East Cobb's demographic, particularly the 40-plus male homeowner segment. The luxury and specialty vehicle angle fits a $118K median income audience better than a standard car show would. Free entry makes it a low-commitment recommendation. Evening hours (5–10 PM) work for families and adults alike. Held at the Cobb Galleria, which is close and familiar to this audience. Solid secondary pick.

blurb:
⭐ **Featured Event: Footloose: The Musical at the Strand**
📅 Friday, May 15 (and weekend run) | Show times vary | Strand Theatre, 117 North Park Square, Marietta
🎟️ Tickets vary | [Get Tickets](https://thestrand.my.salesforce-sites.com)

The Strand Theatre on Marietta Square is one of those places East Cobb residents drive past all the time but do not always make time for. This weekend is a good reason to finally walk through the door. Footloose: The Musical brings the whole story to the stage, complete with the songs you already know by heart. It is a crowd-pleaser in the best possible way, the kind of show that leaves everyone in a good mood on the drive home. Perfect for a teen who needs dragging off the couch, or for two people who just want a fun night out on the Square.
scoring_notes: The Strand is a Marietta landmark and community theater experiences resonate well with East Cobb's engaged, educated audience. Footloose has broad generational appeal, which works well for a mixed audience of established families with teens and empty nesters. Slightly lower uniqueness score because community theater runs multiple weekends. Good secondary pick if the Greek Festival or Braves are already featured. The 'teen drag-along' angle is genuinely useful for this audience.

event_name: Atlanta Braves Block Party at The Battery
blurb:
⭐ **Featured Event: Braves Block Party at The Battery**
📅 Saturday, May 16 | 4:00–8:00 PM | The Battery Atlanta, Cumberland
🎟️ Free | [Learn More](https://www.mlb.com/braves/fans/block-party)

The Battery is throwing a pregame block party Saturday before the Braves take on the Red Sox, and it costs nothing to show up. Themed activities, photo ops, and fan experiences fill the plaza from 4 to 8 PM. Even if you do not have game tickets, this is a solid excuse to head over, grab dinner at one of the Battery restaurants, and soak up the energy of a packed house rivalry weekend. It is the easiest kind of Saturday night: free to walk in, great atmosphere, zero planning required.
scoring_notes: The Battery is a gathering spot East Cobb residents use regularly. Free entry lowers the barrier for families and makes it a compelling add-on to the Braves series weekend. Slightly lower uniqueness score because Battery events happen regularly, but the Red Sox rivalry context and the free price point make it stand out this particular weekend. Works best as a companion feature to the Braves series pick rather than the solo featured event.

venue: Marietta Cobb Museum of Art, Marietta
blurb:
⭐ **Featured Event: Art of the Cocktail**
📅 Friday, May 15 | Evening | Marietta Cobb Museum of Art, Marietta
🎟️ Tickets required | [Get Tickets](https://mariettacobbartmuseum.org)

The Marietta Cobb Museum of Art turns Friday night into something worth getting dressed for. Art of the Cocktail is their signature adult evening event: cocktails, hors d'oeuvres, and a walk through the museum's current exhibitions. Proceeds support the museum, so you are doing something good while having a great time. If you have been meaning to check out the Georgia Wood Artists juried show currently on display, this is the best possible way to do it. Grab a drink, take your time, and make a proper night of it.
scoring_notes: Strong fit for the educated, higher-income East Cobb audience that gravitates toward arts and cultural experiences. The museum is local and the adult evening format appeals to both empty nesters and couples whose kids are older. Price is unknown from the candidate data but the museum's typical pricing would be well within reach for this demographic. The benefit component (proceeds to the museum) resonates with community-minded readers.

event_name: Atlanta Braves vs. Boston Red Sox
blurb:
⭐ **Featured Event: Braves vs. Red Sox at Truist Park**
📅 Friday–Sunday, May 15–17 | Game times vary | Truist Park, Cumberland
🎟️ Tickets from $20+ | [Get Tickets](https://www.mlb.com/braves/schedule/2026/fullseason)

The Braves are leading the NL East by 9 games and are the first team in baseball to hit 30 wins this season. This weekend they host the Boston Red Sox for a three-game series at Truist Park, right in your backyard. Red Sox fans travel, which means the crowd has extra energy, and a Braves win over a historic rival hits different. Grab seats for any of the three games, or pair it with the free Braves Block Party at The Battery on Saturday from 4–8 PM before first pitch. If there was ever a weekend to catch a home game, this is it.
scoring_notes: Truist Park is literally in East Cobb's backyard and Braves fandom runs deep in this community. The Red Sox series adds an element of rivalry that elevates it above a standard home game. The AJC reported the Braves lead the NL East by 9 games and are the first to 30 wins, which makes it a genuinely exciting moment in the season. $118K median income means ticket prices are not a barrier at all. Works for families with teens as well as couples and empty nesters.
